title: 10 best herbal creams for burning feet

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with burning feet, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like athlete's foot.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in aloe vera can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be causing the burning sensation on the feet, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal.

Moisturizing: Aloe vera's hydrating properties can help lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and reduce irritation caused by cracked heels or dry skin, which are common causes of burning sensations on the feet.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and cooling properties. The gel inside the aloe plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids, which soothe and calm the skin. The cream helps to reduce inflammation and cool the skin, providing immediate relief from burning sensations. Its moisturizing properties also hydrate and soften the skin, allowing it to heal and recover from irritation and dryness. This makes aloe vera creams an effective remedy for burning feet.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, specifically aloin and aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly acemannan, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from further damage, alleviating burning feet.

Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins, such as aloctin A and aloctin B, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most used part due to their high content of aloe-emodin, a compound that helps soothe and calm skin irritation.
Gel from Leaves: Aloe vera gel is extracted from the leaves and is widely used for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in relieving burning sensations.

Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: Aloe vera juice extracted from the leaves is used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and soothe burning sensations on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of aloe vera gel in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a separate double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to the melted aloe vera gel and mix well for 5 minutes.
Stir the aloe vera gel mixture into the beeswax and coconut oil mixture until fully incorporated for 10 minutes.
Pour the herbal aloe vera cream mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, also known as pot marigold, has been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it effective in soothing burning sensations on the feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in calendula cream can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to burning sensations on the feet.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: Calendula cream is known for its moisturizing and emollient effects, helping to soothe dry, cracked skin and reduce irritation, which can exacerbate burning sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with burning feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with burning feet. By promoting healthy skin regeneration and tissue repair, Calendula creams provide long-lasting relief from discomfort and pain, allowing individuals to enjoy greater mobility and comfort in their daily lives.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids found in Calendula officinalis help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet.

Carotenoids (specifically Beta-Carotene and Lutein): Carotenoids in Calendula officinalis cream have antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the sensation of burning or discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with burning feet, promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties help soothe and protect the skin.
Leaves: They contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, aiding in the relief of burning sensations.
Stems: Like the flowers and leaves, stems are also a good source of triterpenoids and other compounds that help alleviate skin irritation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula officinalis flowers and dry them further in low heat for 1 hour.
Combine 100g of dried flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a glass jar and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Mix 50g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes and pour it into jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with burning feet, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, thereby reducing burning sensations and promoting skin health.
Soothing and calming properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a sedative effect on the skin, calming irritated tissues and reducing feelings of burning, itching, and tingling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. The cooling and calming effects of chamomile creams can provide relief from burning sensations, itching, and discomfort associated with burning feet. Additionally, chamomile's antiseptic properties help to prevent infections and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing burning feet.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with burning feet by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from burning sensations.

Luteolin: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoid, luteolin helps with burning feet by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which may contribute to burning sensations in the feet.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol helps with burning feet by creating a soothing and calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which may provide relief from burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds, making them suitable for calming and reducing inflammation in burned skin.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, the seeds help to soothe and protect the skin from further irritation and promote healing.

Leaves: Although not as commonly used as flowers or seeds, the leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contain some anti-inflammatory compounds that can contribute to soothing and calming effects when used in creams for burned feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried flowers in their entirety to ensure optimal oil content and fragrance retention.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for full extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container to separate the oil.
Mix the extracted oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create a smooth cream base.

remedy_properties: Cooling and analgesic properties: Menthol in peppermint oil helps to numb the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and inflammation caused by burning feet.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The terpenes present in peppermint oil have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that may contribute to burning sensations on the feet.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with burning feet because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredient, melaleuca oil, has natural decongestant and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm burning sensations. When applied topically, the cream can reduce inflammation and eliminate fungal infections that can cause burning feet. Additionally, the cooling sensation of tea tree oil can provide instant relief from discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals who experience burning feet.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Terpineol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with burning feet by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Cymene: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections that can cause burning sensations in the feet.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have a cooling effect on the skin, reducing heat and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are rich in tea tree oil, which is a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to soothe and relieve burning feet.
Leaves: Tea tree oil from the leaves has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing of burns and other skin irritations.
Leaves: The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of the leaves' tea tree oil help to reduce inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 50g of beeswax pellets and 50g of shea butter in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds.
Add 10g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for 10 seconds.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
Label and package the herbal cream, which is now ready for use after 2 hours of cooling time.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation caused by burning, allowing for quicker healing and relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lavender cream inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery.

Soothing and Calming: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate burning sensations in the feet, and provide overall relaxation and comfort to the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This natural remedy helps to cool and soothe the skin, providing instant relief from burning sensations. As a result, individuals can experience a significant reduction in burning foot discomfort, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and soothe nerve endings, providing relief from burning sensations in the feet.
Linalyl acetate: As another major terpene in Lavandula angustifolia, it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may contribute to burning feet.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with burning feet, while also promoting wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their calming and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and cool the skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting skin comfort.
Stems: Contain essential oils with analgesic and antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve pain and discomfort in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 70 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 days.
Combine 1 cup dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a dark glass container with a lid, shaking daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and mix with 1/2 cup of beeswax, whipping until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet, alleviating burning sensations.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger creams, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to skin tissues, leading to a reduction in burning foot symptoms.

Warming and analgesic properties: Ginger's natural warming effect, combined with its analgesic properties, helps increase blood flow and reduces pain perception in the feet, providing instant relief from burning sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and warming properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the skin. The warming sensation from the cream can also increase blood flow to the affected area, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort. Regular use of ginger cream may help to soothe and comfort burning feet, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease burning sensations associated with conditions such as peripheral neuropathy.

Shogaols: Shogaols, another class of compounds found in ginger, have antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to burning sensations in the feet.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, although not exclusive to ginger, are often present in ginger creams. They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote healing in the skin and tissues, thereby alleviating burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe burning feet.
Roots: Roots are used for their similar properties to rhyzomes, as they also contain gingerols and shogaols that help reduce inflammation and provide relief from burning sensations.
Stems: Stems are used for their high content of essential oils, which are known to have a cooling effect and help alleviate burning and itching sensations in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and 500 milliliters of distilled water for infusion.
Steep the zingiber officinale root in boiling water for 10 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix 200 grams of emulsifying wax and 100 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler to melt.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted wax and butter mixture to create a smooth cream base.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation and swelling in the skin, relieving burning sensations caused by conditions like peripheral neuropathy or nerve damage.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to skin tissues, reducing discomfort and pain associated with burning feet.

Pain-relieving: Turmeric contains a compound called curcuminoid, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort caused by burning sensations in the feet.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, providing relief from burning sensations. Turmeric cream also improves blood circulation, which helps to reduce heat and discomfort in the feet. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent infections and promote a healthy environment for skin healing, making it an effective remedy for burning feet.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with burning feet by reducing inflammation and pain due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC helps with burning feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, which contributes to the development of burning sensations.
Voltilin: Voltilin helps with burning feet by modulating the pain response and reducing inflammation through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used because they are the main storage organ of Curcuma longa, containing high concentrations of bioactive compounds like curcumin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: They are used because they contain a high amount of starch and other compounds that can help soothe and calm skin irritations, making them beneficial for skin care products.
Roots: They are used because they are a rich source of bioactive compounds, including curcuminoids, which have been traditionally used to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse curcuma longa rhizomes in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl to remove solids.
Combine 1 part beeswax with 3 parts infused curcuma longa oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax melts.
Pour the curcuma longa cream mixture into small glass jars and let it cool to set.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain caused by burning feet, making it more comfortable to wear shoes or walk.
Cooling and analgesic properties: Peppermint's menthol content provides a cooling sensation that helps numb the skin, reducing burning sensations and providing instant relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint's antimicrobial compounds help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause burning feet, such as athlete's foot or toenail fungus, by preventing their growth and spread.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with burning feet because of its cooling and soothing properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has a natural analgesic effect that reduces inflammation and numbs the pain in the feet. It also has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to cool the skin and reduce the sensation of burning. Additionally, peppermint creams can help to improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a terpene that helps with burning feet by providing a cooling sensation, reducing inflammation, and blocking nerve pain signals, thus providing quick relief from burning sensations.

Rosmarinus acid (Rosmarinic acid) (phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid helps with burning feet by reducing inflammation, inhibiting pain pathways, and possessing antioxidant properties, which ultimately provide long-term relief from burning sensations.
Menthone: Menthone is a terpene that helps with burning feet by reducing inflammation, numbing the affected area, and inhibiting pain signals, thus providing temporary relief from burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are used to make creams for burning feet due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected area.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain menthol, which helps to cool and numb the skin, providing relief from burning sensations.
Leaves: The leaves also contain menthone, which has a local anesthetic effect, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried mentha x piperita leaves and 10g of beeswax in a stainless steel bowl.
Combine 20g of jojoba oil and 10g of mentha x piperita essential oil in a separate bowl.
Heat the jojoba oil and mentha x piperita essential oil mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Mix the heated oil mixture with the beeswax mixture and 2g of vitamin e oil in the stainless steel bowl.
Pour the mentha x piperita cream mixture into a glass jar and let it cool for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage cream helps reduce inflammation in the feet, which can contribute to burning sensations by reducing swelling and relieving pressure on nerve endings.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and tissues, leading to burning feelings, by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage cream help dry up sweat and reduce moisture in the feet, which can exacerbate burning sensations caused by fungal or bacterial infections, athlete's foot, or excessive sweating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cooling effect of the cream can soothe burning sensations, providing relief from discomfort. The herb's antioxidants also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can alleviate burning feet caused by minor skin irritations or fungal infections. Regular application of Salvia officinalis cream can lead to improved skin health and reduced burning sensations, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid (phenolic compound): As a potent antioxidant, rosmarinic acid helps reduce inflammation and soothe burning sensations in the feet by neutralizing free radicals and promoting blood flow.
Carvacrol (terpene): Carvacrol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate burning pain in the feet by blocking pain receptors and reducing inflammation.

Ursolic acid (triterpenoid): Ursolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antinociceptive properties, which help relieve burning pain in the feet by reducing inflammation, blocking pain receptors, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are the most commonly used part to make creams for burning feet due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for their antifungal properties, which help to soothe and heal skin irritations caused by burning feet.
Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of skin tissue affected by burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 125ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material.
Weigh 50g of beeswax and 25g of emulsifying wax in a double boiler for melting.
Combine 100g of the infused oil with 25g of distilled water and 10g of vitamin e oil in the double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antiseptic and antibacterial properties: Thyme cream's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation, making it effective in treating burning feet caused by fungal or bacterial infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with burning feet caused by conditions like peripheral neuropathy or gout.

Antifungal properties: Thyme cream's antifungal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of fungi like Candida, can help treat fungal infections that cause burning feet, such as athlete's foot or fungal nail infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with burning feet because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol content in thyme creams helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with burning feet. Additionally, thyme's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause burning sensations on the feet. This natural remedy provides relief from discomfort and itchiness, promoting a healthy and pain-free foot environment. Regular use can lead to long-lasting relief.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is a terpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate burning sensations by reducing inflammation and protecting the skin from oxidative damage.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat fungal and bacterial infections causing burning feet, while also reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm burning feet.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used in creams for burning feet due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Stems: Thyme stems are occasionally used in creams for their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections and promote healing in burns and blisters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Boil the mixture for 5 minutes then let it steep for 30 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and mix it with 50g of emulsifying wax to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with burning feet because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the skin. The natural ingredients in marshmallow creams, such as glycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by dry skin and cracked heels. They also help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief from burning sensations. Additionally, marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, leaving feet feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow creams contain high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated skin, reducing the sensation of burning.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in marshmallow creams, such as marshmallow root and chamomile, possess anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet, which can contribute to burning sensations.

Emollient and humectant properties: Marshmallow creams are rich in emollients like glycerin and ceramides, which help to lock in moisture and repair the skin's natural barrier function, reducing dryness and itchiness that can lead to burning.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm burning skin while providing hydration.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers and dry them properly in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind dried marigold petals into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered marigold with 100 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the oil absorbs the herbal properties.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature before transferring it into airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with burning feet because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, glycyrrhizin can help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, which often cause burning sensations on the skin. Additionally, licorice creams also contain soothing compounds like aloe vera and chamomile, which work together to calm irritated skin and provide long-lasting relief from burning sensations. As a result, herbal licorice creams can bring welcome comfort to individuals experiencing discomfort and pain in their feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and pain in the feet caused by conditions like athlete's foot or fungal infections.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The herbal licorice cream's antibacterial and antifungal compounds, such as glycyrrhizic acid and triterpenes, combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause burning sensations on the feet.

Moisturizing properties: Licorice cream's emollients and humectants help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin on the feet, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by conditions like eczema or dry skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Bark: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like glycerrhetic acid, it helps to reduce swelling and pain caused by burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a mixing bowl.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 200 milliliters of distilled water and stir until a smooth paste forms.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to activate the herb's medicinal properties.
Add 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax to the cooled mixture and whip until fully incorporated.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and let it mature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with burning feet because of its natural cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from the burning sensation. Additionally, fennel's astringent properties help to dry up excess moisture, which can exacerbate burning feet conditions. By applying fennel creams directly to the affected area, individuals can experience quick and effective relief from burning feet, allowing them to feel comfortable and relaxed once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal fennel creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with burning feet, providing relief from discomfort.
Anodyne: Fennel's anodyne properties make it a natural pain reliever, capable of soothing and calming the nerves, helping to alleviate burning sensations in the feet.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of fennel can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may contribute to burning feet, promoting healthy skin and reducing irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Seeds: Containing anethole, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in abundance and rinse them thoroughly under cool running water.
Chop 50 grams of the leaves finely and mix with 20 milliliters of pure organic coconut oil in a small bowl.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours at room temperature before straining it through a cheesecloth to extract essence.
Combine 30 milliliters of the extracted essence with 10 grams of beeswax pellets and heat gently over low flame until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool completely before sealing tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with burning feet because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as geraniol and citronellol, work to reduce inflammation and ease pain caused by conditions like plantar fasciitis or athlete's foot. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of geranium creams help to lock in hydration and protect the skin from further irritation, providing long-lasting relief for burning feet.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and antibacterial properties: Geranium essential oil has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to reduce the risk of infection, prevent further irritation, and promote healing in case of open sores or wounds on the feet.

Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation associated with burning feet, providing relief from discomfort and promoting relaxation.

Cooling and soothing properties: Geranium cream has a cooling and soothing effect on the skin, which helps to calm the burning sensation, reduce redness and itching, and provide immediate relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing burning foot pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of geraniin, rhyzomes exhibit antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing in burned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems in the early morning for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and soak it in a cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.
Combine the strained liquid with 100g of unscented coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into sterilized glass jars, storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with burning feet because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce heat and discomfort. The creams also have antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing of cracks and cuts on the skin. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has natural warming properties, which can provide temporary relief from pain and numbness associated with burning feet. By soothing and calming the skin, these creams can bring much-needed comfort and relief to individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Cinnamon's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, provide relief from pain and reduce inflammation in the feet, soothing burning sensations.

Antioxidant Activity: Cinnamon's rich antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to nerve endings, which may contribute to burning foot sensations.
Vasodilatory Effects: Cinnamon's ability to dilate blood vessels improves circulation, which can help reduce heat and discomfort in the feet by promoting healthy blood flow.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming burned skin.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, which helps to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark powder with 100ml of distilled water in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 80°c.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan and melt it over low heat for 5 minutes.
Gradually add the cooled cinnamomum verum mixture to the melted beeswax mixture while stirring constantly until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with burning feet because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as saponins and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort caused by conditions like plantar fasciitis or peripheral neuropathy. Additionally, burdock root has natural antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage, promoting healthy healing and regeneration of skin cells.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polyacetylenes and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and soothe itching, burning, and discomfort caused by conditions like eczema, psoriasis, or contact dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the skin from free radical damage, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to burning feet symptoms.

Demulcent properties: Burdock's mucilages create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, soothing and moisturizing dry, cracked skin, and providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by conditions like eczema, psoriasis, or athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are used to create creams that soothe and calm inflammation in the skin, making them effective against burning sensations.
Seeds: The seeds of the burdock plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce redness and swelling caused by burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry arctium lappa roots according to traditional methods taking approximately 2 weeks.
Combine 200g of dried arctium lappa root powder with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture reaches 60°c.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping into a smooth cream.
Fill sterilized containers with the herbal cream and store in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with burning feet because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and kaempferol, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet. Additionally, the natural astringent properties of dandelion extract help to draw out excess moisture and toxins from the skin, providing long-lasting relief and comfort to those affected by this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce swelling and irritation in the feet, alleviating burning sensations.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and pain in the feet.

Exfoliative and moisturizing properties: Dandelion cream's ability to gently exfoliate and moisturize the skin helps remove dead skin cells and restore the natural barrier function of the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can cause burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems.
Dry the taraxacum officinale plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind 20g of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the taraxacum officinale powder with 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of beeswax.
Stir the mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with burning feet because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as chamazulene and borneol, work to reduce swelling and redness associated with burning sensations. Additionally, yarrow's cooling and antiseptic properties help to calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. By applying yarrow cream directly to affected areas, individuals can find relief from the distressing symptoms of burning feet.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow creams contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in burned areas, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.
Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The cream's active compounds, such as achillein and saponins, have been shown to numb the skin, reducing pain associated with burning sensations on the feet.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by inflammation or burns, which can lead to further damage and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to alleviate burning sensation on the skin.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of phenolic compounds, which help to reduce inflammation, pain, and itching associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated measuring approximately 20 grams.
Combine harvested leaves with 50 milliliters of organic coconut oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler heating the mixture for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate solids from liquids discarding solids and retaining 60 milliliters of infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin e oil in a bowl whipping until smooth and creamy taking approximately 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with burning feet because of its remarkable ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's allantoin content promotes collagen production, reducing inflammation and pain associated with burning sensations. Additionally, comfrey's emollient properties help lock in moisture, easing dryness and discomfort. As a natural anti-inflammatory, comfrey creams can effectively alleviate burning feelings caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy, athlete's foot, or eczema, providing quick relief and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can reduce swelling and pain caused by burning feet, providing relief from discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's allantoin and other saponins promote wound healing by accelerating cell regeneration, reducing scarring, and improving skin elasticity, which can help soothe and repair burned skin on the feet.

Antioxidant properties: Comfrey cream's antioxidant compounds, such as caffeic acid, neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to skin cells, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healthy tissue regeneration in burned areas of the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are rich in allantoin, a natural compound that helps promote skin regeneration and soothe irritation.
Barks: The barks of the comfrey plant contain salicin, a mild pain reliever that can help alleviate discomfort associated with burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried symphytum officinale herb, witch hazel, coconut oil, beeswax, and distilled water for preparation.
Combine one cup of dried symphytum officinale herb with two cups of boiling water in a large glass bowl and steep for 20-30 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding the solids, and set aside to cool for 10-15 minutes.

In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water, melt one-quarter cup of beeswax and combine with three tablespoons of coconut oil, two tablespoons of witch hazel, and the cooled herbal infusion mixture, stirring until well combined.
Remove the cream mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before transferring it to a clean glass jar or tin container for storage and use within six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for burning feet are topical ointments infused with natural herbs and ingredients to soothe and cool the skin. These creams help to alleviate burning sensations, reduce inflammation, and promote healing, providing relief from conditions such as athlete's foot, fungal infections, and heat-related burns. Examples of helpful herbal creams include aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which improve lives by allowing individuals to resume their daily activities with comfort and confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to burning feet that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing soothing ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can provide relief from itching, redness, and inflammation. These creams may also be used to treat minor burns, wounds, and scrapes by promoting healing and reducing pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with burning feet is often a blend of Aloe Vera, Tea Tree Oil, and Calendula. Aloe Vera soothes and cools the skin, while Tea Tree Oil has antibacterial properties that help prevent infection. Calendula, rich in antioxidants, accelerates the healing process and reduces inflammation. By applying these creams together, you can provide your skin with the necessary nutrients to heal and repair itself, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with burning feet. Regular use can lead to long-term relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Neurological sciences : official journal of the Italian Neurological Society and of the Italian Society of Clinical Neurophysiology, a four-week, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial found that a 10% Ajwain topical cream significantly reduced feet burning scores in patients with neuropathic pain, with a 0-10 cm visual analog scale showing a notable decrease in burning intensity. The study randomly assigned 92 patients with daily and nocturnal burning feet to either the Ajwain cream or a placebo, and found significant reductions in numbness, tingling, and allodynia in the Ajwain group compared to the placebo group. As a result, the Ajwain cream is a good candidate for alleviating feet burning, a common neuropathic complication, making herbal creams a valuable option for those experiencing burning feet.
